videos.transcript: Welcome to Pashupatinath Temple. I am in the Outer Complex. Now we will see how you can have darshan here. How much time will it take? When does aarti happen? We will know everything in detail. Just stay in the video and keep saying, Har Har Mahadev. Pashupatinath temple is situated in Nepal's capital Kathmandu. That's why you can reach Kathmandu by flight or bus. And you can go to your hotel by taxi or bus from the airport. Pashupatinath Temple is only 2 km away from the airport. Let me show you my stay in Kathmandu. This is my room here. I will also tell you the location and price. It has double bed and you need AC in summer. But in winter, you need hot AC. Your rooms remains hot that is a good thing. This is in the Thamel area which is the most popular stay location in Kathmandu. This hotel room costs me Rs 6000-7000 per night. But you can also choose a cheaper option. You can stay here for 2,000 rupees or 1,500 rupees. But if you want a good hotel, then you should spend 2,000 to 2,500 rupees. If you want to visit Pashupatinath Temple, then you can stay here. There is another location near the temple but if you stay here you will get Nepal vibes. If your budget allow, then you can stay here. If your budget is low, there are some dharamshalas near the temple. Because I reached here in the afternoon and temple is closed in the afternoon. So, I could not visit the temple during the day. That'swhy it is important that you should know the temple timings. Which we will know in the video later. But in the evening, there is another option. It is evening now. I have come to Pashupatinath Temple. I have come here to see the aarti because aarti happens in evening. How it is and how much there is difference in aarti campared to India. The evening aarti of Pashupatinath Temple is behind the temple. But there is a lot of crowd for the aarti. So if you want to watch this aarti from front then you have to do pre-booking of it. We will tell you the details of the aarti later. There is a bhaav in the aarti. It is a aarti with songs. It is a proper aarti with the harmonium and tabla. It will start soon. This aarti of Pashupatinath Temple starts at 6 pm. It lasts for 30 to 35 minutes. You can also perform the puja before the aarti. I enjoyed the aarti very much At the end, those who were sitting inside get rudraksh mala. If you want to part of the whole aarti, if you want to sit here in the sitting if you book package through Triptotemples then you will get this included it in. So contact them and done your booking and watch this wonderfull aarti. Har Har Mahadev! Next morning around 11 am, I reached the temple once again and to reach here, you can use a bus or a car As soon as you enter the temple's corridor, you can see a lot of prasad shops. I will show you the virtual tour of the Pashupatinath temple. This is the main door, but this is the first one, I will show you the second gate as well. As soon as you enter, you will see such a shivlings. Here you can see the Shankaracharya Math, the mandap and some temples, you can see a lot of small temples. There is only one way to get in and out of the temple. You can say there are only two ways to get in and out of the temple. As soon as you enter, you will see a place This is the Maha Isnaan Bhawan, no worship is allowed here. And you will many pigeons here and that yellow color gate is second gate. You can enter from both these gates. As soon as you enter, you won't see any prasad shops. You can buy anything you want from outside. Now we will come to the main complex. You can enter from here or from there you will come here. As soon as you enter, you will see the main temple of Pashupatinath. There is a bank here and some counters and here pooja charges are written. People sit here before or after darshan. Washroom is also there. The temple of Pashupatinath is right in front of you. So temple is in the big area and I want to show you one more thing. This is the main place of pooja-path. But there is a pattern of darshan in Pashupatinath which we know through pandit ji here. I have done 54 times Kailash Yatra I will give you some information about the devotees coming to the temple. Lord Pashupatinath is counted in the 12 Jyotirlingas If you say Pashupatinath, it means all animals are considered as gods Some people have said that if you have darshan of Nandi before darshan of Pashupatinath, you will become a animal that is not right. Before having darshan in Pashupatinath Temple, people come here for a Pooja and then take a sankalp, you can see behind me and then go to temple for Darshan. I am doing this tour through Trip To Temples. So if you are coming here and want to do pooja path and arrangement of pandit ji, they will done for you. If you want to do a tour in Nepal or Kathmandu, they will arrange everything and for Darshan too. If you want to do it, you can contact them. Rest, we will go for Pooja path and then we will go for Darshan. In Pashupatinath Temple, free wheelchairs are also available for old or disabled people. Behind the temple, there is a river called Baghmati. And here, where there is aarti in the evening, there is also a temple with Shivling. But from this place, you will also get to know a special thing. The place where I have come now, this is the back part of Pashupatinath Temple. You can see the temple on the top. And this is the same temple, which is known as Mukti Isthambh and Mukti Mandap. You can see the entire complex of the temple from here, you can see it from a distance. And there is a ghat below. Here, you will get to see the only temple where there is such a big temple of Lord Shiva and this is where cremation also takes place. You will get to see cremation all over this place. And the river flowing here is the Baghmati River. Apart from this, in the evening, you can see the entire complex here. Aarti takes place here. So, if you want to see aarti, you can come here in the evening. But if you come here, you will feel a little different. Because there will be cremation and all, you will see all this in the back part of the temple. And in the evening, cremation takes place here. Temple is here and aarti takes place here. So, there is a pleasant atmosphere, spiritual atmosphere. And it is said that here, you get liberation from cremation. So, this is a very important place. In Pashupatinath Temple, apart from Rudra Abhishek, you can do a lot of pooja. Which has different official charges. And its receipt is also issues in the Nepal SBI Bank in the complex of this temple. By the way, there is no entry fee for Indians in the temple. But for foreigners, they have to pay Rs. 1000. And even in that, they are not allowed to go to the main temple complex. To visit Pashupatinath Temple, you do not have any VIP option. I mean, officially, there is no facility for VIP Darshan here. It is equal for everyone anyone can come and do Darshan as per the queue. So, if you are thinking that there will be a VIP system, then it is not here. And the months of Sawan, special months of God, you will get a lot of crowd there. Do keep two days for Darshan in Pashupatinath Temple. On the first day, you can have aarti or Darshan. On the second day, you can cover other places of Kathmandu. But most of the people also go to Pokhara and Muktinath in Nepal. And keeps 5-7 days for the entire trip. And you will get the link of the separate videos of all these places in the description box. Taking package for this Nepal trip is the best option Which includes hotel, food, transportation, pooja-path, aarti arrangements. There is a lot of confusion regarding the timing of darshan in Pashupatinath temple and let me make it very clear to you how it is here. The temple opens from around 4.30 in the morning and then the temple remains open till 2 in the afternoon. There is a special puja in between 9.30 to 11.30 so you can also be a part of it. And the temple closes around 2 and then it opens directly at 5 in the evening. And after opening at 5 PM, the temple remains open till 6.30 PM. So according to this timing, you make your plan and do darshan. And my recommendation would be to come early in the morning and do darshan. At that time, you will get the least crowd. So what you are seeing behind me is the main complex of Pashupatinath temple. There is no camera or mobile allowed inside it. So I can't show you inside. But what I can see from outside is a huge statue of Nandi ji. So let's go inside and do darshan. How much time it will take and how to darshan let's know everything in detail. Har Har Mahadev! There is not such any expense of darshan for Pashupatinath temple. But the main expense is of coming from India to Kathmandu by bus or flight accomodation and meals expense is there. If you keep 2 days for here and come by bus from delhi then it costs around Rs 6000 for round trip. While for hotels on double sharing basis stay it costs around Rs 1500 PP and on food Rs 3000. So it will cost around Rs 10,000 on one person. If you take a round trip flight from delhi on normal days then this same budget will cost around Rs 18,500. I have done my darshan in Pashupatinath temple. And let me tell you how I did my darshan. So when you enter inside, you will see a huge statue of Nandi ji and have darshan. After that, you will go inside the temple. I think the doors and roof of the temple are made of silver. And then there is Panchmukhi Shivling here. So when you go inside, you do darshan from 3 to 4 sides. But when you go outside, you come from the other side and you do darshan there. And you offer prasad there. Otherwise, prasad will not be offered inside. And important thing you cann't touch the shivling. So it is like this. And there are many more temples inside, you will see very old temples When I went to Lingraj temple in Bhubaneswar. I saw something like that. And along with that, there is Mukti Mandap. There is a temple of Krishna ji, Ganesh ji, Gorakhnath. There are many other temples too so have darshan of all spent time inside and then come after doing darshan. So you will also have a very nice darshan when you come here. And I will also tell you the time it took to do darshan. It took only 15-20 minutes and it done very easily. The early you come the better darshan you can get. They say that singing and dancing is also happening inside in the morning. So you can see that too. So the sooner you come in the morning, the easier the darshan will be. So when you come, do share your experience in the comment box and do come here. See you in the new vlog. Till then, Har Har Mahadev. Jai Pashupatinath ji.

recommendations: You can see the temple on the top
Taking package for this Nepal trip is the best option Which includes hotel, food, transportation, pooja-path, aarti arrangements
And my recommendation would be to come early in the morning and do darshan

guest houses and traditional noiri homes converted into Charming bnbs Kathmandu airport tips tribu van International Airport is your gateway to kandu upon arrival expect a busy yet manageable experience exchange some currency at the airport for better rates and consider arranging for a hotel pick pick up for convenience a taxi ride to the city center should cost about 10 USD day one Spiritual Awakening and historical exploration morning sambath stupa begin your journey in Kathmandu with a sunrise visit to the sambath stupa an iconic symbol of Nepal's spiritual Heritage known as the monkey temple this ancient religious complex offers a Serene atmosphere and panoramic views of the city the early morning light bathed the stupa in a golden hue creating a magical setting for meditation and photography entry fee approximately 2 USD after descending from swayambunath enjoy a hearty breakfast at Himalayan Java Coffee located in theel their extensive menu features excellent coffee freshly baked pastries and Hearty options like the Himalayan breakfast expect to spend around 5 to 7 USD for a fulfilling breakfast afternoon historical tour of durbar square and Kumar bahal post breakfast head towards the heart of Kathmandu durbar Square this UNESCO world heritage site is a Marvel of noiri architecture and is home to palaces temples and Courtyards the square is alive with history and culture offering an immersive experience into nal's past entry fee 10 USD just a short walk from durbar square is the Kumar bahal the residence of the living goddess Kumari witnessed the unique tradition of worshiping a young prepubescent girl as a living deity remember to respect local customs and guidelines while visiting for lunch visit BN shagar known for its authentic noiri Cuisine try the noiri set a platter of traditional dishes for an unforgettable gastronomic experience average cost per meal 8 to 10 USD evening spiritual visit to pasu panth Temple as the evening of approaches make your way to pasupatinath Temple a sacred Hindu Shrine dedicated to Lord Shiva located along the banks of the bagmati river this Temple complex is a blend of architectural Beauty and spiritual energy witness the evening RT ceremony an enchanting experience filled with chants and rituals entrance fee 10 USD conclude your day with a traditional napali dinner at Bohan Gira set in a restored Palace this restaurant offers an authentic dining experience with cultural performances a typical meal includes a variety of local dishes costing around 15 to 20 USD night Serenity at bodna stupa end your night with a visit to the bodnath stupa one of the largest stupas in the world and an important pilgrimage site for Buddhists the stupa beautifully lit up at night offers a peaceful Ambiance for contemplation and relaxation surrounding the stupa are numerous monasteries and Tibetan shops perfect for some Serene exploration entrance is free but donations are appreciated Insider tip while at durbar Square don't miss the intricate wood carvings and the historic tleu temple day two embracing the heartbeat of Kathmandu morning a tranquil Dawn at the Garden of Dreams begin your morning in the heart of Kathmandu at the Garden of Dreams this historical Garden a rem of Nepal's past offers a serene escape from the city's Buzz stroll through the beautifully manicured Lawns past Edwardian Pavilion and alongside vibrant flower beds the atmosphere here especially in the early hours is peaceful almost meditative for breakfast the on-site Kaiser Cafe provides a perfect blend of east and west their continental breakfast at around 7 USD served in the midst of this Urban Oasis makes for a delightful start to the day remember this is not just a garden it's a portal to the kth Mandu of yester years afternoon the Mel's vibrant pulse after breakfast head to the vibrant streets of theel this area is a Labyrinth of shops cafes and artisanal stores it's a place where the spirit of Kathmandu comes alive with every step the colorful handycrafts the aroma of street food and the murmur of aund conversations create an Ambiance that's uniquely nepes for lunch Rose Mary kitchen and coffee shop offers a cozy retreat with its delectable chicken biryani for around 6 USD the Dish's rich flavors are a testament to the city's cultural diversity late afternoon immersion and festivals depending on your timing you might be lucky enough to witness one of kathmandu's vibrant festivals these events are a kaleidoscope of colors sounds and emotions be it the color frenzy of holly or the light filled evenings of tar or Diwali participating in these festivals is a deep dive into the heart of nepes culture the communal joy and traditional rituals are not just a feast for the eyes but also a soul enriching experience Insider tip if visiting during a festival make sure to check local event schedules many festivals have special ceremonies or parades that are mustsee events local guides or your accommodation hosts can provide the best advice on where and when to experience erience these cultural highlights early evening savoring local bruise in the early evening embark on a culinary Adventure by tasting traditional nepales beverages like Shang and tongba found in the local bars of Kathmandu these drinks offer a glimpse into the traditional Brewing methods of Nepal the experience is not just about the taste but also about understanding local Customs for instance tongba is typically consumed hot making it a unique bever experience priced at around 2 to 4 USD for dinner thei Kitchen in theel is an unmissable stop their thali set a traditional nepes meal is a harmonious blend of flavors and textures representing the culinary Heritage of the thali people this meal costing about 10 USD is not just food it's a cultural Journey Through the diverse tastes of Nepal Knight thrilling encounters with Cobras as night descends brace yourself for an encounter with Cobras handled by traditional snake Charmers on the streets this experience a blend of awe and adrenaline showcases an ancient tradition still alive in Kathmandu well it's heart pounding remember to maintain a safe distance and respect these majestic creatures Insider tip for the Cobra encounter it's advisable to go with a local guide or someone familiar with the practice they can ensure a safe experience and provide insights into the significance of snake charming in nepes culture additionally they can guide you to the most reputable Charmers who treat their animals ethically day three historical treasures and cultural immersion morning Chung Yu Naran Temple begin your day with a visit to the ancient chungu Naran temple one of the oldest Hindu temples in Nepal nestled to top a hill in the Kathmandu Valley it offers a Serene atmosphere and stunning wood carvings depicting various deities the temple complex dating back to the 4th Century provides a profound sense of history and spirituality entrance fee to USD near the temple enjoy a traditional nepes breakfast at a local tea shop sample a steaming cup of Nepali chai and a l Paratha potato stuffed bread a hearty start to your day estimated cost 3 USD inside tip arrive early at shangu Naran to experience the temple in peaceful solitude and witness the locals performing morning rituals afternoon paton's living Heritage head to patan known for its Rich cultural heritage and exquisite craftsmanship explore the bustling streets aligned with traditional noiri houses and visit patan Museum to witness a stunning collection of nepes art entrance fee 5 USD indulge in a relaxing lunch at the patang cafe a Charming eery known for its cozy Ambiance and fusion Cuisine try their chicken Momo nepes dumplings and a fresh salad a delightful Fusion of local and international flavors estimated cost 10 USD evening Buck tapur is timeless Beauty journey to buak tapur a UNESCO world heritage site renowned for its well-preserved medieval art and architecture wander through the narrow Alleyways explore the famous neapa temple and Marvel at the intricate wood carvings and pottery Bak tapur durbar square is a Testament to the city's Rich history and artistic Heritage entrance fee 15 USD for dinner neapa restaurant in bakt tapur offers a rooftop dining experience with views of the city's ancient architecture Savor their traditional nari dishes like bar lentil pancakes and yuck Curry estimated cost 12 USD night reflective moments in theel return to kandu and spend your evening in theel this vibrant neighborhood is perfect for leisurely walks shopping for souvenirs and reflecting on your enriching journey through Nepal's historical wonders inside our tip in bakt tapur don't miss the chance to try Juju Dao a local yogurt known as the king of yogurts for a unique culinary experience best time of year to visit Kathmandu the ideal times to visit Kathmandu are during the Spring march to May and Autumn September to November Seasons spring offers mild weather vibrant blooms and excellent conditions for outdoor activities while Autumn boasts clear skies spectacular Himalayan views and culturally Rich festivals like desan and tar while the monsoon season June to August brings heavy rains and Lush Landscapes it can hinder travel plans winter December to February is quieter and cooler suitable for those who prefer a less crowded experience despite the cold and that wraps up our incredible 3-day journey through Cofe Mandu from the ancient beauty of sambath to the Lively streets of theel we've experienced the heart and soul of this mesmerizing City remember those Insider tips we shared use them to make your own adventure in kandu even more special if you loved exploring with me don't forget to like subscribe and hit the notification Bell for more exciting travel content until our next adventure keep wandering and discovering the magic our world has to offer farewell and see you soon on another thrilling Journey

Embark on a captivating journey to the vibrant city of Kathmandu, Nepal, as we unveil the top things to do and see in this culturally rich destination.

Discover the architectural wonders of Kathmandu's UNESCO World Heritage Sites. Visit the famous Pashupatinath Temple, one of the most sacred Hindu temples in the world, and witness the rituals and ceremonies that take place along the banks of the holy Bagmati River. Explore the intricate beauty of Boudhanath Stupa, an iconic symbol of Buddhism, and soak in the peaceful ambiance as you walk around the stupa and spin prayer wheels.

Immerse yourself in the vibrant atmosphere of Kathmandu's bustling markets. Wander through the narrow streets of Thamel, the city's popular tourist hub, and explore a treasure trove of shops, boutiques, and street vendors offering unique handicrafts, clothing, and souvenirs. Indulge in the flavors of Nepali cuisine by trying local street food and traditional dishes like momo and dal bhat.

Experience the spiritual side of Kathmandu by visiting the ancient temples and monasteries that dot the city's landscape. Explore the serene Swayambhunath, also known as the Monkey Temple, and enjoy panoramic views of Kathmandu Valley from its hilltop location. Marvel at the intricate woodwork and art at the Kathmandu Durbar Square, a complex of palaces, temples, and courtyards that reflects the city's rich history.

Escape the urban chaos and find tranquility in the surrounding nature. Take a day trip to the nearby Kathmandu Valley and explore the picturesque towns of Bhaktapur and Patan, known for their well-preserved medieval architecture and traditional Newari culture. Visit the nearby Nagarkot hill station for stunning sunrise and sunset views over the Himalayas.

Join us as we uncover the hidden gems and must-visit attractions of Kathmandu, the cultural heart of Nepal. Let us transport you to a world of ancient traditions, breathtaking architecture, and unforgettable experiences.

Get ready to be captivated by the allure of Kathmandu in this immersive video experience. So, pack your bags, and let the adventure begin in this remarkable city where spirituality, history, and warm Nepali hospitality await!

Combine it with some friendly locals, and you get an unforgettable trip. So, let me show you the different shades of Kathmandu. Today's guide is hello. >> Sua is my friend's cousin, a local of Kathmandu, and was nice enough to show me around. It's always amazing to go on tours and see things from a historical and information perspective. But the experiences that you get by spending just a day with a local is so different. And that is why although she met me in Dbar Square, which is the main tourist attraction in the city, we did not stay there. We jumped on a local bus and headed to Bhaktapur. I don't need to say that it was a bumpy ride. Took us two hours to get there. I paid 500 rupees and entered the UNESCO World Heritage site of Paktapur. Now, if you're thinking what's so special about it, well, Paktapur is like an open air museum and it feels like you have traveled back in time. I'm a history nerd, so this place was like a gold mine for me. While we were walking towards the main square, so I just spotted a tiny almost hidden shop with simple wooden bench outside serving Momo. There was no fancy decor, no menu board. We went in and ordered my first plate of real Nepalese momo. We ordered a plate of Joel Momo which is a staple of Nepal and you absolutely have to try if you're going there. This is my soul food. I was dying to try this and yes honestly you don't get this taste in India. I was absolutely mind blown by how good it was and I did not know this was just the beginning of Nepalese cuisine blowing my mind. After a tummy and hearts full, we walked over to explore the Bhaktapur Darbar Square. As we went deeper, I realized that Bhaktapur is not just another stop in the Gmandu Valley. The Navars were master builders, artists, and traders. The people behind the valley's iconic pagora temples, intricate wood carvings, and vibrant festivals. videos.description:
Experience the vibrant chaos and rich history of Kathmandu, Nepal, with our comprehensive city guideвҖ”crafted after spending 11 days of in-depth exploration and countless hours of planning. This video covers every essential detail for travelers: how to get there, visa requirements, the best times to visit, safety tips, and how to navigate the cityвҖҷs maze-like streets. Discover KathmanduвҖҷs unique blend of medieval architecture and modern energy as we guide you through must-see sites like ThamelвҖҷs bustling markets, the spiritual Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple), the sacred Pashupatinath Temple, the mesmerizing Boudhanath Stupa, and the historic Kathmandu Durbar Square. We also break down practical topics like using ATMs, cash versus credit cards, language tips, and what to expect from local food, accommodations, and transportation.

Whether youвҖҷre a first-time visitor or a seasoned adventurer, this guide will help you make the most of your Kathmandu experience. Get honest advice on where to stay, how to avoid common pitfalls, and which local dishes (like momo and dal bhat) are worth trying. We also highlight the best day trips from Kathmandu, including Patan, Bhaktapur, Nagarkot, and Shivapuri National Park, ensuring you donвҖҷt miss the Himalayan views or NepalвҖҷs cultural treasures. With a touch of humor and real-world tips, this video is your go-to resource for planning an unforgettable trip to NepalвҖҷs captivating capital.
